mechanism: The current mixed shock environment (residual supply-side inflation + demand softening) prevents either demand-dominant or supply-dominant regime from locking in, producing the observed oscillation between -0.2 and +0.4 correlation
regime_dependence: Holds when shock mix is ambiguous; resolves when one shock type clearly dominates

mechanism: Anchored inflation expectations flatten the Phillips Curve, allowing the Fed to look through supply shocks (producing negative correlation). De-anchored expectations steepen the Phillips Curve, forcing the Fed to tighten into weakness (producing positive correlation). Expectations anchoring determines the effective shock classification.
regime_dependence: Holds across all regimes; the anchoring state determines which regime prevails

mechanism: Fiscal supply pressure ($2T+/year issuance) drives term premium at the long end, producing positive 30Y-SPX correlation, while front-end rates still reflect monetary policy expectations (negative 2Y-SPX correlation). The bifurcation is a direct manifestation of partial fiscal dominance.
regime_dependence: Holds under partial fiscal dominance; under full fiscal dominance, the bifurcation would collapse as 2Y-SPX also turns positive

mechanism: Compressed ROIC-WACC spreads make equity valuations ~1.5-2.0x more sensitive to rate increases through the fundamental earnings channel. This amplifies the contradiction between equity markets pricing negative correlation (bonds as insurance) and rates markets pricing positive correlation (term premium), because the fundamental damage from positive correlation is larger than historical analogues.
regime_dependence: Amplification effect is strongest when spreads are compressed (<5pp); at wider spreads (>8pp), the relationship weakens

mechanism: As $2.5T of ZIRP-era debt refinances at 5.5-6.5% (vs. original 2.5-3.5% coupon), incremental interest expense mechanically compresses ROIC-WACC spreads further, particularly for the S&P 493. This creates a time-stamped, foreseeable amplifier of equity rate sensitivity.
regime_dependence: Concentrated in 2025-2027 maturity window; attenuated if Fed cuts rates significantly or private credit absorbs refinancing

mechanism: The two-dimensional corridor (core inflation 2.0-3.5% × fiscal deficit 4.5-7.0% GDP) prevents correlation regime resolution by keeping both demand-dominant and supply-dominant forces active simultaneously. Exit from the corridor on either dimension would resolve the regime.
regime_dependence: Regime instability occurs only while parameters remain inside the corridor boundaries

mechanism: The structural standoff between fiscal expansion and monetary tightening extends correlation transitions to 5-7 years (vs. 12-36 months for clean monetary transitions). The current 4-year transition is within the fiscal stalemate analogue range, suggesting 1-3 more years.
regime_dependence: Holds when fiscal and monetary authorities are pulling in opposite directions; resolves if policy alignment occurs

mechanism: When the asset price channel works counterproductively (AI rally loosening conditions despite hiking), the effective Taylor coefficient is reduced because rate hikes do not transmit to financial conditions as expected. The Fed's revealed preference coefficient of 1.0-1.2 partly reflects this transmission breakdown.
regime_dependence: Strongest when equity market rallies are driven by factors orthogonal to monetary policy (e.g., AI)

mechanism: The same fiscal deficits ($2T+/year) that drive term premium higher (pushing long-end correlation positive) also channel ~$400-600B annually into corporate profits, sustaining near-term earnings. The earnings resilience and the multiple compression share a single fiscal cause.
regime_dependence: Operates whenever fiscal deficits are large enough to materially affect both Treasury supply and corporate profit pools (roughly >4% GDP)

mechanism: Fiscal dominance makes the central bank reaction function endogenous: the Fed cannot credibly commit to φ_π > 1.5 when rate hikes increase interest expense on $35T+ debt, potentially destabilizing the fiscal trajectory. This constrains the effective coefficient to ~1.0-1.2.
regime_dependence: Binding when debt/GDP is high enough that interest expense is a material share of the budget (current ~125% debt/GDP)

mechanism: Geopolitical escalation triggers immediate and permanent fiscal commitments (defense spending, supply chain duplication, sanctions compliance infrastructure) that expand the structural fiscal deficit, reinforcing fiscal-driven positive correlation pressure.
regime_dependence: Activated by geopolitical escalation events; the fiscal commitment persists even after the triggering event partially normalizes

mechanism: Large carry trade positioning ($500B-$1T) converts gradual correlation shifts into abrupt flips through forced deleveraging. Yen strengthening triggers margin calls on yen-funded positions that simultaneously hit equity and bond markets, producing rapid positive correlation spikes.
regime_dependence: Operative when USD/JPY carry positioning is large and BOJ is normalizing; amplifier effect rather than independent cause

mechanism: The inconsistency between equity pricing (assuming negative correlation) and rates/term premium pricing (reflecting positive correlation) is the market-observable manifestation of the bimodal distribution. Different asset classes price different modes of the distribution.
regime_dependence: Persists as long as the regime is transitional and the distribution remains bimodal

mechanism: If index-level correlation primarily reflects mega-cap tech behavior rather than broad equity dynamics, the apparent cross-asset pricing inconsistency may be partially explained by market-cap-weighting artifacts rather than genuine mispricing across asset classes.
regime_dependence: Effect is proportional to market concentration; currently ~35% in top 10 stocks, exceeding historical norms

mechanism: Crowded positioning for positive correlation creates contrarian asymmetry: if a growth shock materializes or positioning unwinds, the forced covering amplifies the negative correlation move. The crowding may explain why the regime oscillates rather than locking into positive territory.
regime_dependence: Relevant when positioning surveys and options skew indicate crowded directional bets; attenuated when positioning is balanced

mechanism: Excluding SBC from non-GAAP earnings systematically understates the true WACC burden on equity valuations by ~25-35%, making the already-compressed ROIC-WACC spread appear wider than its economic reality. This compounds the fundamental rate sensitivity.
regime_dependence: Most impactful for tech-heavy indices where SBC/revenue ratios are highest
